page: Amazon Web Services c9g.large is a compute-optimized server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. With a 1:1 ratio of physical cores to vCPUs, each core runs a single thread, ensuring dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading overhead. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

The c9g.large instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, which minimizes virtualization overhead to maximize resource availability. It does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

As an arm64-based compute-optimized instance, the c9g.large offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores and lower memory-to-CPU ratios. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a streamlined choice for lightweight compute tasks. It is suited for workloads such as web serving, microservices, containerized applications, and basic CI/CD pipelines that do not require massive memory footprints or local disk I/O.
description: Amazon Web Services c9g.large is an arm64 compute-optimized server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. Running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Designed for qualitative cost efficiency in compute-focused workloads, the c9g.large is suited for containerized microservices, lightweight web serving, and distributed application nodes.
